comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Armenia episode. Books, cookbooks, history, travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE ARMENIA COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4b9CNWr

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN ARMENIA*
  ◦ Day trips to Khor Virap, Geghard, and Lake Sevan → https://shorturl.at/NfFIw
  ◦ Browse all Armenia experiences → https://shorturl.at/NnFcN

*📖 PLAN YOUR ARMENIA TRIP*
  ◦ Bradt Armenia, the only standalone English language guide to the country, 6th edition → https://amzn.to/4xKyydN
  ◦ Eastern Armenian dictionary and phrasebook, over 3,000 entries with a concise grammar and pronunciation guide → https://amzn.to/4aln4mR

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO ARMENIA*
  ◦ A Concise History of the Armenian People, the standard one volume history from ancient times to the present → https://amzn.to/3ShDvuq
  ◦ My Name Is Aram by William Saroyan, the classic stories of an Armenian family in California → https://amzn.to/4aLdeLe

*🍲 BRING ARMENIA HOME*
  ◦ Lavash, the acclaimed cookbook of Armenian bread and the dishes that surround it → https://amzn.to/4eEllu3
  ◦ Armenia: Art, Religion, and Trade in the Middle Ages, the Metropolitan Museum volume with 282 color plates → https://amzn.to/3QwLkfb

replies.author: @Yesica1993
replies.text: It is not possible to film and post a new documentary every couple of days.

comments.author: @avakianaleco8604
comments.text:
The Armenian people have a history of survival spanning 5,000 years—and dating back as far as 8,000 years—under various names such as Armanum, Subartu, Areni, Portasar, Hayassa, and Urartu. 

Armenians is one of oldest people on the planet. All peoples have changed their names and empires, have come and gone. Armenia stands, as it does every year and every century, like a Christmas tree.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That continuity is remarkable to sit with — few peoples can trace an unbroken thread that far back. Thank you for adding that depth here. What part of that history do you feel doesn't get told enough?

comments.author: @vahankupalian2412
comments.text: 36 letters became 39

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Good catch on the detail — the Armenian alphabet started with 36 letters under Mesrop Mashtots, and three more ( o, f, and ev) were added later as the language evolved. Appreciate you flagging the nuance.
